english: Indeed, Allah is with the patient.
reflection: Every hardship is temporary. Patience is not passive — it is an active trust in Allah's perfect timing. The patient man does not merely endure; he believes.

arabic: وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُ مَخْرَجًا
english: And whoever fears Allah — He will make for him a way out.
reflection: Taqwa is not just avoidance of sin — it is a constant consciousness of Allah that opens doors you never knew existed.

english: For indeed, with hardship will be ease.
reflection: The scholars noted this is repeated twice in the same surah. Difficulty is singular; ease is promised in abundance. You are closer to relief than you think.

arabic: وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ وَكِيلًا
english: And put your trust in Allah. And sufficient is Allah as Disposer of affairs.
reflection: True reliance on Allah does not mean abandoning effort. It means acting with full effort while knowing the outcome belongs to Him alone.

english: And do not despair of relief from Allah.
reflection: Prophet Yaqub said this while separated from both his sons for decades. Despair is forbidden for the believer — mercy can arrive at any moment.
Dhikr is the nourishment of the heart. The one who remembers Allah abundantly is never truly alone, regardless of circumstances.

english: Verily, in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find rest.
reflection: No amount of worldly achievement will settle the restless heart. Its cure was given to us directly — remembrance. Start there.

arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
english: Indeed, Allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good.
reflection: Nothing done sincerely for Allah is wasted. Not a single prayer at dawn, not a single act of kindness — it is all recorded and will be returned in full.

english: And say: My Lord, increase me in knowledge.
reflection: This is the only du'a in the Quran where Allah directly tells the Prophet to ask for more of something. It signals the immense value Allah places on knowledge.

arabic: وَمَا أُوتِيتُم مِّن شَيْءٍ فَمَتَاعُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتُهَا
english: Whatever you have been given is but the enjoyment of worldly life and its adornment.
reflection: This life is a brief way station. Build with intention, invest for the akhirah, and hold worldly possessions lightly.

english: Indeed, Allah loves those who rely upon Him.
reflection: Tawakkul is not weakness — it is the highest form of strength. It says: I have done my part, and I trust the All-Knowing with the rest.

arabic: خُذِ الْعَفْوَ وَأْمُرْ بِالْعُرْفِ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْجَاهِلِينَ
english: Take the easier course, enjoin kindness, and turn away from the ignorant.
reflection: Three pillars of noble character in one verse: forgiveness, goodness, and composure. This is the mark of a man of substance.
